name: Frank Herbert - Dune
reason: A sweeping epic of politics, ecology, and religion set on the desert planet Arrakis

name: Ursula K. Le Guin - The Left Hand of Darkness
reason: A groundbreaking exploration of gender and society on the planet Gethen

name: Isaac Asimov - The Gods Themselves
reason: A masterwork exploring contact with a parallel universe and the nature of scientific progress

name: Arthur C. Clarke - Rendezvous with Rama
reason: A mysterious alien spacecraft enters the solar system, discovered by human explorers

name: William Gibson - Neuromancer
reason: The defining cyberpunk novel that popularized the concept of cyberspace

name: Orson Scott Card - Ender's Game
reason: A gifted child is trained through strategic war games to defend Earth against alien threat

name: Lois McMaster Bujold - Barrayar
reason: A tale of political intrigue and personal courage in a spacefaring empire

name: Neil Gaiman - American Gods
reason: A mythic road trip across America where ancient gods clash with new deities of technology and media

name: Cixin Liu - The Three-Body Problem
reason: First contact with an alien civilization unfolds against the backdrop of China's Cultural Revolution

name: N.K. Jemisin - The Fifth Season
reason: On a world wracked by catastrophic seismic activity, a woman seeks her missing daughter amidst apocalypse

name: Arkady Martine - A Memory Called Empire
reason: A diplomat from a small space station must navigate the intrigue of a vast galactic empire

name: Robert Jackson Bennett - The Tainted Cup
reason: A murder mystery in a fantastical empire where magic and biology intertwine
